title: Thickly Creamy Ramekin British Sirloin Pie And Mash
description: A rich, comforting British classic featuring tender plant-based sirloin-style steak smothered in a deeply emulsified, gooey cream gravy, baked in individual ramekins under golden puff pastry and served with smooth buttery mash.

ingredients.name: plant-based sirloin steak strips
ingredients.notes: high-quality vegetarian beef alternative, cut into bite-sized pieces

ingredients.name: unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: divided for the filling emulsion and the mash

ingredients.name: heavy cream
ingredients.notes: used to mantecare-emulsify the rich gravy

ingredients.name: russet potatoes
ingredients.notes: peeled and quartered for the mash

ingredients.name: cheddar cheese
ingredients.notes: finely grated to enhance the gooey texture

instructions: Boil the potatoes in salted water for fifteen minutes until they are completely fork-tender.
Sear the vegetarian sirloin strips in a hot pan with twenty-five grams of butter until they are browned on all sides.
Pour in the vegetable stock and simmer gently for five minutes to reduce the liquid by half.
Whisk in the heavy cream and remaining butter vigorously off the heat to emulsify the sauce into a thickly creamy gravy.
Stir the grated cheddar cheese into the hot gravy until it melts completely and becomes beautifully gooey.
Divide the creamy steak filling equally among four individual ramekins.
Cut the puff pastry into four circles slightly larger than the ramekins, place them over the filling, and brush with beaten egg.
Bake the ramekins at two hundred degrees Celsius for twenty minutes until the pastry is puffed and golden brown.
Mash the drained potatoes thoroughly with the remaining butter and a splash of cream until they are perfectly smooth.
Serve each piping hot ramekin pie alongside a generous scoop of the warm buttery mash.
